behaviors.description: Valid JSON payloads containing markdown-heavy strings parse successfully and proceed through normal hook input handling.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is parser-to-hook-input-handoff across local modules (MOCK-MISS yes). No required real OS/network/subprocess (REAL-INFRA no), no AI dependency, no multi-agent chain. System was considered but rejected because behavior text says only valid payloads should parse and proceed through normal handling, not real process behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real parser + hook input handler in-process. Feed valid JSON fixtures whose string fields contain code fences, backticks, and `$vars`. Assert parse succeeds and downstream handler receives expected structured payload without error traces.

behaviors.description: Truly malformed JSON payloads produce a `json_parse_failed` trace record with enough context to diagnose the malformed section.
behaviors.justification: Behavior targets interaction between parse failure detection and trace-record construction/sink (module boundary). No real subprocess/OS required by text; malformed bytes can be injected in-process. Higher levels rejected because behavior does not require real process execution or external infra.
behaviors.test_description: Run parser + trace pipeline in-process with malformed JSON cases (unterminated strings, bad commas, broken braces). Assert a `json_parse_failed` record is emitted and includes diagnostic context fields (e.g., offset/snippet/error message) sufficient to localize the malformed section.

behaviors.description: The local error-policy path maps parse failure to a handled result (no throw) and marks telemetry intent.
behaviors.justification: This is local policy mapping logic on a parse-failure input to a handled output + telemetry intent flag. It can fail within one function/object boundary with no I/O. Integration considered but rejected because behavior text explicitly says local error-policy path.
behaviors.test_description: Call the error-policy mapper directly with a representative parse-failure object. Assert it returns handled/no-throw result and sets telemetry-intent marker as expected.

behaviors.description: In real process execution, malformed stdin does not crash the hook process and still emits the expected trace signal before exit.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires real process execution and stdin/exit semantics (REAL-INFRA yes). In-process fakes can miss crash/exit-ordering and signal-before-exit failures (MOCK-HIDE yes).
behaviors.test_description: Spawn the hook process as a subprocess, write malformed stdin, capture exit code and emitted trace output/artifact. Assert process does not crash unexpectedly, emits expected parse-failure trace signal, then exits cleanly.

behaviors.description: A real hook invocation path (stdin -> parser -> trace sink) produces the expected trace artifact when malformed input is supplied.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text says real invocation path and includes stdin; minimum failure boundary is end-to-end process I/O path to real trace artifact emission. Integration rejected because the stated real path can fail due to runtime wiring/order/stream handling not visible in pure in-process tests.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the hook through its real invocation entrypoint with malformed stdin and configured trace sink destination. Assert the expected trace artifact is created/populated with parse-failure signal.

behaviors.description: Regression tests distinguish malformed-JSON failures from valid-markdown payloads, preventing false attribution to markdown content itself.
behaviors.justification: Needs comparative coverage across parser + failure-attribution/trace behavior using two fixture classes (valid-markdown JSON vs malformed JSON). No required real OS/subprocess in behavior text. System considered but rejected because no explicit real-process condition is stated.
behaviors.test_description: Create a regression suite with paired fixtures: (A) valid JSON containing markdown-heavy strings and (B) malformed JSON. Run through parser+attribution path in-process. Assert only (B) emits parse-failure traces and (A) follows success path, preventing markdown false positives.
